title: sapindus verticillatus
intro: Sapindus verticillatus, also known as the soapbark tree, is a deciduous tree native to eastern Asia, characterized by its smooth, gray bark and large, pinnate leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sapindus verticillatus include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
